criteria_verification.criterion_text: `src/jarvis/agents/subagents/jarvis_reasoner.py` exposes a module-level `graph: CompiledStateGraph` via `create_deep_agent(model="openai:jarvis-reasoner", system_prompt=<resolved>, tools=[])`. Compilation happens at import time per DDR-012 — `from jarvis.agents.subagents.jarvis_reasoner import graph` returns a compiled object without further initialisation.
criteria_verification.evidence: Module-level binding `graph: CompiledStateGraph[Any, Any, Any, Any] = _build_graph()` runs at import. _build_graph() instantiates a shared chat-model wrapper via init_chat_model('openai:jarvis-reasoner') (no network call) and then compiles one create_deep_agent inner graph per RoleName with kwargs `model=model, tools=[], system_prompt=ROLE_PROMPTS[role], subagents=[]`. The outer StateGraph wraps the three role dispatchers and is returned compiled. Verified by tests/test_subagents_jarvis_reasoner.py::TestAC001GraphCompiledAtImport (4 tests, including isinstance(graph, CompiledStateGraph), module attribute check, __all__ export check, and the REASONER_MODEL constant assertion) and re-confirmed in turn 2 by re-running pytest (31 tests pass). Coach independently verified this AC on turn 1.
criteria_verification.notes: Module-level binding `graph: CompiledStateGraph[Any, Any, Any, Any] = _build_graph()` runs at import. _build_graph() instantiates a shared chat-model wrapper via init_chat_model('openai:jarvis-reasoner') (no network call) and then compiles one create_deep_agent inner graph per RoleName with kwargs `model=model, tools=[], system_prompt=ROLE_PROMPTS[role], subagents=[]`. The outer StateGraph wraps the three role dispatchers and is returned compiled. Verified by tests/test_subagents_jarvis_reasoner.py::TestAC001GraphCompiledAtImport (4 tests, including isinstance(graph, CompiledStateGraph), module attribute check, __all__ export check, and the REASONER_MODEL constant assertion) and re-confirmed in turn 2 by re-running pytest (31 tests pass). Coach independently verified this AC on turn 1.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: The first node of the graph reads `input["role"]` (or equivalent initial-state channel), looks up `ROLE_PROMPTS[RoleName(role)]`, and injects it as the system prompt.
criteria_verification.evidence: _resolve_role is registered as the entry point via builder.set_entry_point('resolve_role'). It reads state.get('role'), wraps the value in RoleName(role_value) for membership validation, and on success sets state['resolved_role']. The conditional-edge router then dispatches to a role-specific node whose pre-compiled inner deep-agent has system_prompt=ROLE_PROMPTS[role] resolved at build time. Verified by TestAC002FirstNodeResolvesRole (parametrized over critic / researcher / planner) and TestAC009LeafGraphInvariants.test_create_deep_agent_called_with_each_role_prompt which asserts the set of system_prompts passed to create_deep_agent equals ROLE_PROMPTS values exactly. Coach verified on turn 1.
criteria_verification.notes: _resolve_role is registered as the entry point via builder.set_entry_point('resolve_role'). It reads state.get('role'), wraps the value in RoleName(role_value) for membership validation, and on success sets state['resolved_role']. The conditional-edge router then dispatches to a role-specific node whose pre-compiled inner deep-agent has system_prompt=ROLE_PROMPTS[role] resolved at build time. Verified by TestAC002FirstNodeResolvesRole (parametrized over critic / researcher / planner) and TestAC009LeafGraphInvariants.test_create_deep_agent_called_with_each_role_prompt which asserts the set of system_prompts passed to create_deep_agent equals ROLE_PROMPTS values exactly. Coach verified on turn 1.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `OPENAI_BASE_URL` environment variable is set to `<config.llama_swap_base_url>/v1` at graph construction; the supervisor process sets this via lifecycle (no hard-coded URL in this module).
criteria_verification.evidence: The jarvis_reasoner.py module never assigns to os.environ and contains no http URL literal in executable code. Verified by TestAC003NoHardCodedBaseUrl which strips the module docstring with a regex, then asserts the executable source contains no `http://` literal, no `promaxgb10`, no `9000/v1`, no `os.environ[`, no `os.environ.setdefault`, and no `os.putenv`. The supervisor lifecycle owns the OPENAI_BASE_URL export side-effect. Coach verified on turn 1.
criteria_verification.notes: The jarvis_reasoner.py module never assigns to os.environ and contains no http URL literal in executable code. Verified by TestAC003NoHardCodedBaseUrl which strips the module docstring with a regex, then asserts the executable source contains no `http://` literal, no `promaxgb10`, no `9000/v1`, no `os.environ[`, no `os.environ.setdefault`, and no `os.putenv`. The supervisor lifecycle owns the OPENAI_BASE_URL export side-effect. Coach verified on turn 1.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Unknown role (e.g. `"bard"`, `"CRITIC"`, `"adversarial"`) returns structured error via `async_tasks` state channel: `"ERROR: unknown_role — expected one of {critic, researcher, planner}, got=<value>"`. **Never raises.**
criteria_verification.evidence: _resolve_role wraps RoleName(role_value) in try/except ValueError; the except branch returns _structured_error with message `ERROR: unknown_role — expected one of {critic, researcher, planner}, got=<repr(value)>` populated on the async_tasks, messages, and error state channels. The conditional-edge router short-circuits to END so no dispatcher runs and no LLM is contacted. Verified by TestAC004UnknownRoleStructuredError parametrized over bard / CRITIC / Critic / adversarial / deep_reasoner plus an explicit test that graph.invoke does not raise. Coach verified on turn 1.
criteria_verification.notes: _resolve_role wraps RoleName(role_value) in try/except ValueError; the except branch returns _structured_error with message `ERROR: unknown_role — expected one of {critic, researcher, planner}, got=<repr(value)>` populated on the async_tasks, messages, and error state channels. The conditional-edge router short-circuits to END so no dispatcher runs and no LLM is contacted. Verified by TestAC004UnknownRoleStructuredError parametrized over bard / CRITIC / Critic / adversarial / deep_reasoner plus an explicit test that graph.invoke does not raise. Coach verified on turn 1.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Empty-string role (`""`) — `RoleName("")` raises `ValueError` at enum lookup; the graph catches and maps to the `unknown_role` branch (ASSUM-004, confirmed).
criteria_verification.evidence: _resolve_role uses `if role_value is None` (identity check, not falsiness), so empty string '' falls through to the RoleName(role_value) call which raises ValueError per default Python enum semantics. The except clause maps onto the unknown_role error string with `got=''`. Verified by TestAC005EmptyStringRoleMapsToUnknownRole asserting `output.startswith('ERROR: unknown_role')` and `"got=''" in output`, plus a non-raising guarantee. Coach verified on turn 1.
criteria_verification.notes: _resolve_role uses `if role_value is None` (identity check, not falsiness), so empty string '' falls through to the RoleName(role_value) call which raises ValueError per default Python enum semantics. The except clause maps onto the unknown_role error string with `got=''`. Verified by TestAC005EmptyStringRoleMapsToUnknownRole asserting `output.startswith('ERROR: unknown_role')` and `"got=''" in output`, plus a non-raising guarantee. Coach verified on turn 1.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Missing role field entirely returns structured error: `"ERROR: missing_field — role is required"`.
criteria_verification.evidence: _resolve_role checks `state.get('role') is None` first, which covers both an absent 'role' key and an explicit None value, and returns _structured_error('ERROR: missing_field — role is required', correlation_id). Verified by TestAC006MissingRoleField with two tests: one omitting the role key, one passing role=None — both assert the output equals the literal error string verbatim. Coach verified on turn 1.
criteria_verification.notes: _resolve_role checks `state.get('role') is None` first, which covers both an absent 'role' key and an explicit None value, and returns _structured_error('ERROR: missing_field — role is required', correlation_id). Verified by TestAC006MissingRoleField with two tests: one omitting the role key, one passing role=None — both assert the output equals the literal error string verbatim. Coach verified on turn 1.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Empty prompt returns structured error: `"ERROR: missing_field — prompt is required"`.
criteria_verification.evidence: _resolve_role uses `if not prompt_value` which treats empty string and missing key the same way, returning _structured_error('ERROR: missing_field — prompt is required', correlation_id). Verified by TestAC007EmptyPromptStructuredError covering both empty-string and missing-key scenarios. Coach verified on turn 1.
criteria_verification.notes: _resolve_role uses `if not prompt_value` which treats empty string and missing key the same way, returning _structured_error('ERROR: missing_field — prompt is required', correlation_id). Verified by TestAC007EmptyPromptStructuredError covering both empty-string and missing-key scenarios. Coach verified on turn 1.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Missing llama-swap alias (adapter-level failure) surfaces as structured error mentioning the `/running` endpoint.
criteria_verification.evidence: _make_role_runner wraps `await deep_agent.ainvoke(...)` in try/except Exception; on failure it emits `ERROR: llama_swap_unavailable — model alias 'jarvis-reasoner' could not be resolved against the llama-swap /running endpoint (role=<role>, detail=<exc>)`. Verified by TestAC008LlamaSwapAliasMissing which builds a synthetic deep agent whose ainvoke raises RuntimeError, runs the dispatcher node, and asserts the output contains both `/running` and `llama_swap_unavailable` and that correlation_id propagates. Coach verified on turn 1.
criteria_verification.notes: _make_role_runner wraps `await deep_agent.ainvoke(...)` in try/except Exception; on failure it emits `ERROR: llama_swap_unavailable — model alias 'jarvis-reasoner' could not be resolved against the llama-swap /running endpoint (role=<role>, detail=<exc>)`. Verified by TestAC008LlamaSwapAliasMissing which builds a synthetic deep agent whose ainvoke raises RuntimeError, runs the dispatcher node, and asserts the output contains both `/running` and `llama_swap_unavailable` and that correlation_id propagates. Coach verified on turn 1.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Graph exposes no tools (`tools=[]`) and no further subagents — leaf per design §8 / DDR-010.
criteria_verification.evidence: Every create_deep_agent call inside _build_graph passes tools=[] and subagents=[] explicitly. Verified by TestAC009LeafGraphInvariants.test_create_deep_agent_called_per_role_with_empty_tools which patches langchain.chat_models.init_chat_model and deepagents.create_deep_agent at the source modules, forces a fresh re-import, and asserts call_count == 3 with kwargs.tools == [] and kwargs.subagents == [] for every call. Coach verified on turn 1.
criteria_verification.notes: Every create_deep_agent call inside _build_graph passes tools=[] and subagents=[] explicitly. Verified by TestAC009LeafGraphInvariants.test_create_deep_agent_called_per_role_with_empty_tools which patches langchain.chat_models.init_chat_model and deepagents.create_deep_agent at the source modules, forces a fresh re-import, and asserts call_count == 3 with kwargs.tools == [] and kwargs.subagents == [] for every call. Coach verified on turn 1.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: Module has no LLM network call at import (FakeListChatModel in tests; production model only hit on actual dispatch).
criteria_verification.evidence: init_chat_model only constructs the chat-model wrapper (per the supervisor pattern verified in TASK-J001-006) and create_deep_agent only assembles a graph — neither issues a network request. Verified by TestAC010NoNetworkCallAtImport which patches the source modules and asserts neither invoke / ainvoke / stream / astream / predict was called on the model object during the forced re-import; plus assert_called_once_with('openai:jarvis-reasoner') on init_chat_model. Coach verified on turn 1.
criteria_verification.notes: init_chat_model only constructs the chat-model wrapper (per the supervisor pattern verified in TASK-J001-006) and create_deep_agent only assembles a graph — neither issues a network request. Verified by TestAC010NoNetworkCallAtImport which patches the source modules and asserts neither invoke / ainvoke / stream / astream / predict was called on the model object during the forced re-import; plus assert_called_once_with('openai:jarvis-reasoner') on init_chat_model. Coach verified on turn 1.

criteria_verification.criterion_text: `correlation_id` from input propagates through to the async task's output channel (scenario: *session correlation identifier propagates from input through to check-task results*).
criteria_verification.evidence: _structured_error and _make_role_runner both copy state.get('correlation_id') into every async_tasks output payload. Verified by TestAC011CorrelationIdPropagates with four tests covering the unknown_role, missing_role, empty_prompt branches plus the None-correlation-id default case. Coach verified on turn 1.
criteria_verification.notes: _structured_error and _make_role_runner both copy state.get('correlation_id') into every async_tasks output payload. Verified by TestAC011CorrelationIdPropagates with four tests covering the unknown_role, missing_role, empty_prompt branches plus the None-correlation-id default case. Coach verified on turn 1.
